endpoints.name: POST /v1beta/apps
endpoints.description: Creates an App Engine application for a Google Cloud Platform project. Required fields: id - The ID of the target Cloud Platform project. location - The region (https://cloud.google.com/appengine/docs/locations) where you want the App Engine application located.For more information about App Engine applications, see Managing Projects, Applications, and Billing (https://cloud.google.com/appengine/docs/standard/python/console/).
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN.

endpoints.name: GET /v1beta/apps/{appsId}
endpoints.description: Gets information about an application.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{APPSID}} with a real appsid.

endpoints.name: PATCH /v1beta/apps/{appsId}
endpoints.description: Updates the specified Application resource. You can update the following fields: auth_domain - Google authentication domain for controlling user access to the application. default_cookie_expiration - Cookie expiration policy for the application. iap - Identity-Aware Proxy properties for the application.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{APPSID}} with a real appsid.

endpoints.name: GET /v1beta/apps/{appsId}/authorizedCertificates
endpoints.description: Lists all SSL certificates the user is authorized to administer.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{APPSID}} with a real appsid.

endpoints.name: POST /v1beta/apps/{appsId}/authorizedCertificates
endpoints.description: Uploads the specified SSL certificate.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{APPSID}} with a real appsid.

endpoints.name: GET /v1beta/apps/{appsId}/authorizedCertificates/{authorizedCertificatesId}
endpoints.description: Gets the specified SSL certificate.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{AUTHORIZEDCERTIFICATESID}} with a real authorizedcertificatesid. Replace {{APPSID}} with a real appsid.

endpoints.name: DELETE /v1beta/apps/{appsId}/authorizedCertificates/{authorizedCertificatesId}
endpoints.description: Deletes the specified SSL certificate.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{AUTHORIZEDCERTIFICATESID}} with a real authorizedcertificatesid. Replace {{APPSID}} with a real appsid.

endpoints.name: PATCH /v1beta/apps/{appsId}/authorizedCertificates/{authorizedCertificatesId}
endpoints.description: Updates the specified SSL certificate. To renew a certificate and maintain its existing domain mappings, update certificate_data with a new certificate. The new certificate must be applicable to the same domains as the original certificate. The certificate display_name may also be updated.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{AUTHORIZEDCERTIFICATESID}} with a real authorizedcertificatesid. Replace {{APPSID}} with a real appsid.

endpoints.name: GET /v1beta/apps/{appsId}/authorizedDomains
endpoints.description: Lists all domains the user is authorized to administer.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{APPSID}} with a real appsid.

endpoints.name: GET /v1beta/apps/{appsId}/domainMappings
endpoints.description: Lists the domain mappings on an application.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{APPSID}} with a real appsid.

endpoints.name: POST /v1beta/apps/{appsId}/domainMappings
endpoints.description: Maps a domain to an application. A user must be authorized to administer a domain in order to map it to an application. For a list of available authorized domains, see AuthorizedDomains.ListAuthorizedDomains.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{APPSID}} with a real appsid.

endpoints.name: GET /v1beta/apps/{appsId}/domainMappings/{domainMappingsId}
endpoints.description: Gets the specified domain mapping.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{DOMAINMAPPINGSID}} with a real domainmappingsid. Replace {{APPSID}} with a real appsid.

endpoints.name: DELETE /v1beta/apps/{appsId}/domainMappings/{domainMappingsId}
endpoints.description: Deletes the specified domain mapping. A user must be authorized to administer the associated domain in order to delete a DomainMapping resource.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{DOMAINMAPPINGSID}} with a real domainmappingsid. Replace {{APPSID}} with a real appsid.

endpoints.name: PATCH /v1beta/apps/{appsId}/domainMappings/{domainMappingsId}
endpoints.description: Updates the specified domain mapping. To map an SSL certificate to a domain mapping, update certificate_id to point to an AuthorizedCertificate resource. A user must be authorized to administer the associated domain in order to update a DomainMapping resource.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{DOMAINMAPPINGSID}} with a real domainmappingsid. Replace {{APPSID}} with a real appsid.

endpoints.name: GET /v1beta/apps/{appsId}/firewall/ingressRules
endpoints.description: Lists the firewall rules of an application.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{APPSID}} with a real appsid.

endpoints.name: POST /v1beta/apps/{appsId}/firewall/ingressRules
endpoints.description: Creates a firewall rule for the application.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{APPSID}} with a real appsid.

endpoints.name: GET /v1beta/apps/{appsId}/firewall/ingressRules/{ingressRulesId}
endpoints.description: Gets the specified firewall rule.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{INGRESSRULESID}} with a real ingressrulesid. Replace {{APPSID}} with a real appsid.

endpoints.name: DELETE /v1beta/apps/{appsId}/firewall/ingressRules/{ingressRulesId}
endpoints.description: Deletes the specified firewall rule.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{INGRESSRULESID}} with a real ingressrulesid. Replace {{APPSID}} with a real appsid.

endpoints.name: PATCH /v1beta/apps/{appsId}/firewall/ingressRules/{ingressRulesId}
endpoints.description: Updates the specified firewall rule.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{INGRESSRULESID}} with a real ingressrulesid. Replace {{APPSID}} with a real appsid.

endpoints.name: POST /v1beta/apps/{appsId}/firewall/ingressRules:batchUpdate
endpoints.description: Replaces the entire firewall ruleset in one bulk operation. This overrides and replaces the rules of an existing firewall with the new rules.If the final rule does not match traffic with the '*' wildcard IP range, then an "allow all" rule is explicitly added to the end of the list.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{APPSID}} with a real appsid.

endpoints.name: GET /v1beta/apps/{appsId}/locations
endpoints.description: Lists information about the supported locations for this service.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{APPSID}} with a real appsid.

endpoints.name: GET /v1beta/apps/{appsId}/locations/{locationsId}
endpoints.description: Gets information about a location.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{LOCATIONSID}} with a real locationsid. Replace {{APPSID}} with a real appsid.

endpoints.name: GET /v1beta/apps/{appsId}/operations
endpoints.description: Lists operations that match the specified filter in the request. If the server doesn't support this method, it returns UNIMPLEMENTED.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{APPSID}} with a real appsid.

endpoints.name: GET /v1beta/apps/{appsId}/operations/{operationsId}
endpoints.description: Gets the latest state of a long-running operation. Clients can use this method to poll the operation result at intervals as recommended by the API service.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{OPERATIONSID}} with a real operationsid. Replace {{APPSID}} with a real appsid.

endpoints.name: GET /v1beta/apps/{appsId}/services
endpoints.description: Lists all the services in the application.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{APPSID}} with a real appsid.

endpoints.name: GET /v1beta/apps/{appsId}/services/{servicesId}
endpoints.description: Gets the current configuration of the specified service.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{SERVICESID}} with a real servicesid. Replace {{APPSID}} with a real appsid.

endpoints.name: DELETE /v1beta/apps/{appsId}/services/{servicesId}
endpoints.description: Deletes the specified service and all enclosed versions.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{SERVICESID}} with a real servicesid. Replace {{APPSID}} with a real appsid.

endpoints.name: PATCH /v1beta/apps/{appsId}/services/{servicesId}
endpoints.description: Updates the configuration of the specified service.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{SERVICESID}} with a real servicesid. Replace {{APPSID}} with a real appsid.

endpoints.name: GET /v1beta/apps/{appsId}/services/{servicesId}/versions
endpoints.description: Lists the versions of a service.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{SERVICESID}} with a real servicesid. Replace {{APPSID}} with a real appsid.

endpoints.name: POST /v1beta/apps/{appsId}/services/{servicesId}/versions
endpoints.description: Deploys code and resource files to a new version.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{SERVICESID}} with a real servicesid. Replace {{APPSID}} with a real appsid.

endpoints.name: GET /v1beta/apps/{appsId}/services/{servicesId}/versions/{versionsId}
endpoints.description: Gets the specified Version resource. By default, only a BASIC_VIEW will be returned. Specify the FULL_VIEW parameter to get the full resource.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{VERSIONSID}} with a real versionsid. Replace {{SERVICESID}} with a real servicesid. Replace {{APPSID}} with a real appsid.

endpoints.name: DELETE /v1beta/apps/{appsId}/services/{servicesId}/versions/{versionsId}
endpoints.description: Deletes an existing Version resource.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{VERSIONSID}} with a real versionsid. Replace {{SERVICESID}} with a real servicesid. Replace {{APPSID}} with a real appsid.

endpoints.name: PATCH /v1beta/apps/{appsId}/services/{servicesId}/versions/{versionsId}
endpoints.description: Updates the specified Version resource. You can specify the following fields depending on the App Engine environment and type of scaling that the version resource uses:Standard environment instance_class (https://cloud.google.com/appengine/docs/admin-api/reference/rest/v1beta/apps.services.versions#Version.FIELDS.instance_class)automatic scaling in the standard environment: automatic_scaling.min_idle_instances (https://cloud.google.com/appengine/docs/admin-api/reference/rest/v1beta/apps.services.versions#Version.FIELDS.automatic_scaling) automatic_scaling.max_idle_instances (https://cloud.google.com/appengine/docs/admin-api/reference/rest/v1beta/apps.services.versions#Version.FIELDS.automatic_scaling) automaticScaling.standard_scheduler_settings.max_instances (https://cloud.google.com/appengine/docs/admin-api/reference/rest/v1beta/apps.services.versions#StandardSchedulerSettings) automaticScaling.standard_scheduler_settings.min_instances (https://cloud.google.com/appengine/docs/admin-api/reference/rest/v1beta/apps.services.versions#StandardSchedulerSettings) automaticScaling.standard_scheduler_settings.target_cpu_utilization (https://cloud.google.com/appengine/docs/admin-api/reference/rest/v1beta/apps.services.versions#StandardSchedulerSettings) automaticScaling.standard_scheduler_settings.target_throughput_utilization (https://cloud.google.com/appengine/docs/admin-api/reference/rest/v1beta/apps.services.versions#StandardSchedulerSettings)basic scaling or manual scaling in the standard environment: serving_status (https://cloud.google.com/appengine/docs/admin-api/reference/rest/v1beta/apps.services.versions#Version.FIELDS.serving_status) manual_scaling.instances (https://cloud.google.com/appengine/docs/admin-api/reference/rest/v1beta/apps.services.versions#manualscaling)Flexible environment serving_status (https://cloud.google.com/appengine/docs/admin-api/reference/rest/v1beta/apps.services.versions#Version.FIELDS.serving_status)automatic scaling in the flexible environment: automatic_scaling.min_total_instances (https://cloud.google.com/appengine/docs/admin-api/reference/rest/v1beta/apps.services.versions#Version.FIELDS.automatic_scaling) automatic_scaling.max_total_instances (https://cloud.google.com/appengine/docs/admin-api/reference/rest/v1beta/apps.services.versions#Version.FIELDS.automatic_scaling) automatic_scaling.cool_down_period_sec (https://cloud.google.com/appengine/docs/admin-api/reference/rest/v1beta/apps.services.versions#Version.FIELDS.automatic_scaling) automatic_scaling.cpu_utilization.target_utilization (https://cloud.google.com/appengine/docs/admin-api/reference/rest/v1beta/apps.services.versions#Version.FIELDS.automatic_scaling)manual scaling in the flexible environment: manual_scaling.instances (https://cloud.google.com/appengine/docs/admin-api/reference/rest/v1beta/apps.services.versions#manualscaling)
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{VERSIONSID}} with a real versionsid. Replace {{SERVICESID}} with a real servicesid. Replace {{APPSID}} with a real appsid.

endpoints.name: GET /v1beta/apps/{appsId}/services/{servicesId}/versions/{versionsId}/instances
endpoints.description: Lists the instances of a version.Tip: To aggregate details about instances over time, see the Stackdriver Monitoring API (https://cloud.google.com/monitoring/api/ref_v3/rest/v3/projects.timeSeries/list).
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{VERSIONSID}} with a real versionsid. Replace {{SERVICESID}} with a real servicesid. Replace {{APPSID}} with a real appsid.

endpoints.name: GET /v1beta/apps/{appsId}/services/{servicesId}/versions/{versionsId}/instances/{instancesId}
endpoints.description: Gets instance information.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{VERSIONSID}} with a real versionsid. Replace {{INSTANCESID}} with a real instancesid. Replace {{SERVICESID}} with a real servicesid. Replace {{APPSID}} with a real appsid.

endpoints.name: DELETE /v1beta/apps/{appsId}/services/{servicesId}/versions/{versionsId}/instances/{instancesId}
endpoints.description: Stops a running instance.The instance might be automatically recreated based on the scaling settings of the version. For more information, see "How Instances are Managed" (standard environment (https://cloud.google.com/appengine/docs/standard/python/how-instances-are-managed) | flexible environment (https://cloud.google.com/appengine/docs/flexible/python/how-instances-are-managed)).To ensure that instances are not re-created and avoid getting billed, you can stop all instances within the target version by changing the serving status of the version to STOPPED with the apps.services.versions.patch (https://cloud.google.com/appengine/docs/admin-api/reference/rest/v1/apps.services.versions/patch) method.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{VERSIONSID}} with a real versionsid. Replace {{INSTANCESID}} with a real instancesid. Replace {{SERVICESID}} with a real servicesid. Replace {{APPSID}} with a real appsid.

endpoints.name: POST /v1beta/apps/{appsId}/services/{servicesId}/versions/{versionsId}/instances/{instancesId}:debug
endpoints.description: Enables debugging on a VM instance. This allows you to use the SSH command to connect to the virtual machine where the instance lives. While in "debug mode", the instance continues to serve live traffic. You should delete the instance when you are done debugging and then allow the system to take over and determine if another instance should be started.Only applicable for instances in App Engine flexible environment.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{VERSIONSID}} with a real versionsid. Replace {{INSTANCESID}} with a real instancesid. Replace {{SERVICESID}} with a real servicesid. Replace {{APPSID}} with a real appsid.

endpoints.name: POST /v1beta/apps/{appsId}:repair
endpoints.description: Recreates the required App Engine features for the specified App Engine application, for example a Cloud Storage bucket or App Engine service account. Use this method if you receive an error message about a missing feature, for example, Error retrieving the App Engine service account. If you have deleted your App Engine service account, this will not be able to recreate it. Instead, you should attempt to use the IAM undelete API if possible at https://cloud.google.com/iam/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts/undelete?apix_params=%7B"name"%3A"projects%2F-%2FserviceAccounts%2Funique_id"%2C"resource"%3A%7B%7D%7D . If the deletion was recent, the numeric ID can be found in the Cloud Console Activity Log.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{APPSID}} with a real appsid.

endpoints.name: GET /v1beta/projects/{projectsId}/locations
endpoints.description: Lists information about the supported locations for this service.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PROJECTSID}} with a real projectsid.

endpoints.name: GET /v1beta/projects/{projectsId}/locations/{locationsId}
endpoints.description: Gets information about a location.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PROJECTSID}} with a real projectsid. Replace {{LOCATIONSID}} with a real locationsid.

endpoints.name: POST /v1beta/projects/{projectsId}/locations/{locationsId}/applications
endpoints.description: Creates an App Engine application for a Google Cloud Platform project. Required fields: id - The ID of the target Cloud Platform project. location - The region (https://cloud.google.com/appengine/docs/locations) where you want the App Engine application located.For more information about App Engine applications, see Managing Projects, Applications, and Billing (https://cloud.google.com/appengine/docs/standard/python/console/).
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PROJECTSID}} with a real projectsid. Replace {{LOCATIONSID}} with a real locationsid.

endpoints.name: GET /v1beta/projects/{projectsId}/locations/{locationsId}/applications/{applicationsId}
endpoints.description: Gets information about an application.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PROJECTSID}} with a real projectsid. Replace {{APPLICATIONSID}} with a real applicationsid. Replace {{LOCATIONSID}} with a real locationsid.

endpoints.name: POST /v1beta/projects/{projectsId}/locations/{locationsId}/applications/{applicationsId}:repair
endpoints.description: Recreates the required App Engine features for the specified App Engine application, for example a Cloud Storage bucket or App Engine service account. Use this method if you receive an error message about a missing feature, for example, Error retrieving the App Engine service account. If you have deleted your App Engine service account, this will not be able to recreate it. Instead, you should attempt to use the IAM undelete API if possible at https://cloud.google.com/iam/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts/undelete?apix_params=%7B"name"%3A"projects%2F-%2FserviceAccounts%2Funique_id"%2C"resource"%3A%7B%7D%7D . If the deletion was recent, the numeric ID can be found in the Cloud Console Activity Log.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PROJECTSID}} with a real projectsid. Replace {{APPLICATIONSID}} with a real applicationsid. Replace {{LOCATIONSID}} with a real locationsid.

endpoints.name: GET /v1beta/projects/{projectsId}/locations/{locationsId}/operations
endpoints.description: Lists operations that match the specified filter in the request. If the server doesn't support this method, it returns UNIMPLEMENTED.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PROJECTSID}} with a real projectsid. Replace {{LOCATIONSID}} with a real locationsid.

endpoints.name: GET /v1beta/projects/{projectsId}/locations/{locationsId}/operations/{operationsId}
endpoints.description: Gets the latest state of a long-running operation. Clients can use this method to poll the operation result at intervals as recommended by the API service.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PROJECTSID}} with a real projectsid. Replace {{OPERATIONSID}} with a real operationsid. Replace {{LOCATIONSID}} with a real locationsid.
